brief_document.title: Engine schedule management API
brief_document.purpose: Expose schedule management through the Engine public API: create, update, pause, resume, delete, list, and describe. Each mutation records the corresponding schedule event and updates the evaluator's state.
brief_document.task: Add schedule management methods to Engine: create_schedule, update_schedule, pause_schedule, resume_schedule, delete_schedule, list_schedules, describe_schedule. Each mutation method records the corresponding event through a schedule-specific Recorder, notifies the ScheduleEvaluator, and returns the result. Query methods (list, describe) project from schedule state.

requirements.title: Engine::create_schedule
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add create_schedule(config: ScheduleConfig) -> Result<ScheduleId, EngineError> that generates a ScheduleId, records ScheduleCreated, arms the evaluator, and returns the id.
acceptance: The evaluator has an armed timer for the new schedule.

requirements.title: Engine update, pause, resume, delete
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add update_schedule (changes config), pause_schedule (disarms timer), resume_schedule (re-arms timer), and delete_schedule (permanently disarms). Each records the corresponding event.
acceptance: update_schedule records ScheduleUpdated and the evaluator uses the new config.
pause_schedule records SchedulePaused and disarms the timer.
resume_schedule records ScheduleResumed and re-arms the timer.
delete_schedule records ScheduleDeleted and permanently disarms.
Operating on a non-existent schedule returns an error.

requirements.title: Engine list_schedules and describe_schedule
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add list_schedules() returning all non-deleted schedules with their state, and describe_schedule(id) returning the full state of a single schedule including trigger history.
acceptance: describe_schedule returns config, is_paused, last_triggered_at, next_trigger_at.
describe_schedule on a non-existent id returns an error.
Deleted schedules do not appear in list_schedules.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement server/gRPC/HTTP endpoints — that is AK-005.
SHALL NOT implement the evaluator internals — those are in AK-003.
verification: Unit tests cover create, update, pause, resume, delete, list, describe, and error paths.

constraints.text: Schedule state is projected from events, never stored as mutable fields.
Schedule evaluation uses durable timers (AT infrastructure), not wall-clock polling.
Overlap and catch-up policies are explicit per-schedule configuration, not implicit engine behavior.
Recovery must reconstruct schedule state from events and re-arm timers for all active schedules.
A deleted schedule's events are retained for audit but the schedule is no longer evaluated.
Schedule mutations are serialized per ScheduleId to prevent concurrent modification races.

resolved_context.intention: Production workflow engines must support time-triggered execution: run this billing workflow every midnight, check this API every 5 minutes, generate this report every Monday at 9am. Without built-in scheduling, every user must build their own cron-to-workflow bridge, duplicating coordination logic (overlap prevention, catch-up, pause/resume) that belongs in the engine. This cluster adds a Schedule resource to Aion: a persistent, durable configuration that starts workflow executions on a cron expression or fixed interval, with policies for overlap, catch-up on missed triggers, and pause/resume. Schedules are engine-level — they survive restarts, are queryable, and their trigger history is auditable.
